concept: Return / Come Back / Go Again to Origin
description: To come or go back to a place previously left, to complete the circle of departure and arrival. 'Turema' carries the satisfaction of completion—the wanderer home, the cycle closed, the familiar regained after absence. Yet the returner is changed; one never returns to quite the same place.
sound_symbolism: The word turns back on itself. 'Tu' sets out with dental clarity, the direction reversed. Then 're' returns with liquid motion, retracing path. Finally 'ma' meets home with nasal warmth, arrival at the origin.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi turema' (I return). As noun: 'wela turema' (good return / welcome homecoming). Takes locative: 'mi mua noma turema' (I return to home). Implies previous departure; one cannot return where one has not left.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cycles of departure and return structure traditional life—seasonal migration, daily rounds, the hero's return transformed.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Returning to breath, returning to awareness, returning to this moment—meditation is continuous return to presence.
